products.name: WARPS (Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System)
products.descriptor: ballistic whole-aircraft recovery parachute system family

evidence.snippet: #### Ballistic Recovery Systems was founded in 1980 by Boris Popov of Saint Paul, Minnesota, after he survived a 400-foot (120 m) fall in a partially collapsed hang glider in 1975. As a result, Popov invented the WARPS, the Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System.

###### SINCE 1980
We have saved over 504 lives

products.name: BRS System for Cessna 172/182
products.descriptor: FAA/EASA Part 23-certified retrofit whole-aircraft parachute for Cessna 172 and 182

evidence.snippet: BRS is the only Whole Aircraft Recovery Parachute System certified as a retrofit for the Cessna 172s and Cessna 182s.

Developed and certified FAA/EASA Part 23 systems for Cessna 172, Cessna 182, Bristell B23, Elixir, Tecnam P-Mentor and others coming.
![](https://brsaerospace.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/03/IPS-Feat-edited.jpg)

products.name: Cirrus Airframe Parachute System (CAPS)
products.descriptor: type-certified whole-aircraft parachute co-developed with Cirrus for SR20/SR22

evidence.snippet:
#### Developed the Cirrus Airframe Parachute System
Collaborated with Cirrus Aircraft to develop the first Recovery Parachute System to be used on a line of type certified aircraft: the Cirrus SR20, followed by the Cirrus SR22 in 2001.
